critic_raw:
You are reviewing an ML project. A payments company built a graph neural network to detect account-takeover fraud...

CRITIQUE:

1. [OPERATING-POINT-MISMATCH] OPERATING POINT MISMATCH: GNN threshold tuned on validation to hit 0.8%; rules engine at legacy threshold that accidentally hit 0.8% due to seasonal volume drop. Operating point asymmetry.

2. [STALE-RULES-SNAPSHOT] STALE RULES SNAPSHOT: Rules were revised by analysts four months before study but the revision was not replayed. The evaluated rules engine uses outdated weights.

3. [REPLACEMENT-CLAIM-STRONGER-THAN-EVIDENCE] REPLACEMENT CLAIM TOO STRONG: One test month during a seasonally low-volume period is insufficient evidence for headcount reallocation.
defender_raw:
You are reviewing an ML project. A payments company built a graph neural network to detect account-takeover fraud...

DEFENSE:

Operational snapshot is the right baseline. Both systems hit 0.8% review rate. GNN shows 15 pp improvement. Operational requirements met.
adjudication_raw: Issues 1 and 2 are genuine and empirically testable — replay updated rule weights at a designed 0.8% threshold on the same test data. Issue 3 is a scope-of-evidence claim that the defender does not address. The direction of the GNN advantage is plausible; the magnitude is uncertain under fair comparison conditions. Empirical test agreed.

empirical_test.measure: Fraud capture rate at 0.8% review-rate threshold on the same test month
empirical_test.success_criterion: GNN fraud capture rate ≥ 70% vs. updated rules engine (revised weights, threshold explicitly set to 0.8%) ≤ 65%
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Gap below 5 pp between GNN and updated, properly-thresholded rules engine
